title: structural_damage
description: An unexpected structural crack is discovered in a load-bearing wall following a minor tremor.

timeline.title: Structural Crack Discovered
timeline.description: Facilities staff discover a significant crack in a load-bearing wall following a minor earth tremor.

timeline.title: Affected Area Cordoned
timeline.description: Immediate cordon established around the affected section. Structural engineer contacted.

timeline.title: Structural Assessment
timeline.description: Structural engineer on-site conducting assessment. Partial evacuation of upper floors ordered.

timeline.title: Risk Rating Issued
timeline.description: Engineer rates the damage as moderate but recommends emergency shoring before re-entry.

timeline.title: Emergency Shoring
timeline.description: Structural shoring team installs temporary supports. Building declared safe for limited use.

timeline.title: Phased Return
timeline.description: Undamaged areas cleared for use. Long-term repair plan developed.

node-1.title: Structural Crack Discovered
node-1.situation: Following a minor earth tremor, a facilities technician has discovered a significant diagonal crack running through a load-bearing wall on the ground floor. The crack is approximately 8mm wide and spans 2 metres. Two hundred staff are currently working in the building above.
node-1.question: What is your immediate response?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately cordon off the area around the crack, restrict access to the floors above the affected wall, and contact a structural engineer for an emergency assessment.

options.label: B
options.text: Note the crack and schedule a routine facilities inspection for next week — minor cracks after tremors are common.

options.label: C
options.text: Have the facilities team fill the crack with sealant and monitor it visually.

node-2.title: Partial Evacuation Decision
node-2.situation: The structural engineer has arrived and is conducting the assessment. Based on the crack's location and width, they recommend evacuating floors 3, 4, and 5 as a precaution while the full assessment is completed. This will displace approximately 120 staff.
node-2.question: How do you manage the partial evacuation?

options.label: A
options.text: Follow the engineer's recommendation immediately — evacuate floors 3–5 via stairwells, establish a remote working arrangement for displaced staff, and await the full structural assessment before deciding on re-entry.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow staff to continue working on the upper floors while monitoring the crack for further movement.

node-3.title: Engineer's Assessment Report
node-3.situation: The structural engineer has completed the assessment. The damage is rated as moderate. The wall has not fully compromised the structure but requires emergency temporary shoring before any re-entry of affected floors. Without shoring, further tremors could cause progressive failure. A shoring contractor is available but will take 4 hours to arrive.
node-3.question: How do you manage the 4-hour wait?

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain the evacuation of upper floors for the full 4-hour period, activate the BCP for displaced staff, and do not allow any exceptions for re-entry regardless of business pressure.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow senior management to re-enter upper floors briefly to retrieve laptops and documents — the structural risk is moderate, not severe.

node-4.title: Emergency Shoring Complete
node-4.situation: The shoring contractor has installed temporary structural supports. The engineer has re-inspected and declared floors 1 and 2 safe for re-entry. Floors 3–5 remain restricted pending permanent repairs. Leadership is asking whether the building should be permanently vacated while repairs are carried out.
node-4.question: How do you advise on building use?

options.label: A
options.text: Follow the structural engineer's clearance — allow use of floors 1 and 2 only, maintain restrictions on floors 3–5, engage a contractor for permanent repairs, and commission a full building structural survey to check for additional damage.

options.label: B
options.text: Declare the whole building safe now that shoring is in place and allow full operations to resume on all floors.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Floors 1 and 2 are safely operational. Displaced staff on floors 3–5 are working remotely. Emergency shoring is in place and a permanent repair contract has been awarded. A full building structural survey is underway. All personnel are safe and the incident is fully documented for insurance.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
